ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
sdk: add support for local versions and optimize sed calls (#7870)

* Added support for local sdk versions

Added support for local versions (prefixed by '+'), so they are filtered out in _listInstallableVersions() and they are displayed correctly by _listAllVersion()

* Optimize sed calls

Co-authored-by: Marc Cornellà <marc.cornella@live.com>

Fast algorithm to determine grep alias flags

This version tries whether grep supports all the flags together
and progressively checks older flags if the grep test fails.
This means only one grep call if all flags are supported, and
one additional call for every flag that's not supported, up to
a maximum of 3 calls.
homestead: repair sed regex (#8103)
change key string

systemadmin: fix header line sorting in pscpu (#6167)

Inside `pscpu` and `pscpu10` aliases, remove sorting of the header line.
feature: add condition for regular expression

Report only active battery (#4726)

On a system with multiple batteries (like thinkpads) report percentage
and time remaining only for the active battery (the one being
discharged).

Ideally we should report all batteries, but acpi only shows time remaining
for the active battery. Also callers of these functions expect a single
return value. This is still better than reporting 596% remaining (like it
did on my laptop).

For the reference, the output of acpi command with multiple batteries looks
like this:

    Battery 0: Unknown, 5%
    Battery 1: Discharging, 86%, 03:14:04 remaining

debian: fix alias completion, rename ag and clean up README (#7698)

* Use double quotes to cache value of $apt_pref and $apt_upgr
* Clean up and fix syntax of command checks
* Clean up README and document $apt_pref/$apt_upgr overriding mechanism
* Rename `ag` alias (apt upgrade) to `au`
* Clean up README and fix syntax

Fixes #3686 
Fixes #4660 
Closes #5906

Co-authored-by: Noah Vesely <fowlslegs@riseup.net>
removed_lines: -    alias ail="sed -e 's/  */ /g' -e 's/ *//' | cut -s -d ' ' -f 1 | "' xargs sudo $apt_pref install'
added_lines: +    alias ail="sed -e 's/  */ /g' -e 's/ *//' | cut -s -d ' ' -f 1 | xargs sudo $apt_pref install"

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
installer: make sure shell in /etc/shells is not commented

Otherwise we risk a situation where a full path to `zsh` is commented, i.e.:

    #/usr/local/bin/zsh

gitfast: update plugin (#7152)

* Stop loading git plugin
* Update completion and git-prompt to v2.10
* Update completion to v2.14

rake-fast: remove brackets from completion entries

Fixes #5653

Fixed `fwl` function in `firewalld` plugin when `sources` used (#7011)

`firewall-cmd --get-active-zones` returns something like this:

```
dmz
  sources: ipset:dmz-hosts
public
  interfaces: eth0
```

if zone binding is based on source ips, so strings with `sources: ...` should be excluded along with `interfaces: ...` to get zones list.

Use existing ssh-agent when invoking a sudo shell (#3891)

When invoking a shell as root using ```sudo -s```, the ssh-agent plugin
starts a new agent although it already exists.

The problem boils down to a check if ssh-agent is running using
```ps x```. If that is extended to ```ps ax``` for root, then the
existing ssh-agent will still work.

rand-quote: update URL

Fixes #6949

Fix mix-fast plugin (#6708)

The mix command for listing all available actions has been changed from
`mix --help` to `mix help`.

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
ssh-agent: check ssh-agent process w/ ps again

The alternative is using tools that aren't available everywhere.
The latest report is that cygwin/msys2 doesn't have pgrep.

Fixes #5418.

Moved git.io url to https

git.io now redirects to https. So the current function break. Changing it to https fixes the issue.
Clobber ssh environment regardless of setopt (#5322)
Fix git_prompt_status() not showing ahead/behind/diverged status correctly (#5388)
[Composer] redirect stderr to /dev/null for completion commands

Put `local var` declaration in its own line in lib/git.zsh

In places, the local statement will override the exit code and the written
command won't have the effect intended when it was written.

For example, when it's not inside a git repo the exit code won't be true,
but the local statement will make it true regardless. See #4708.
removed_lines: -    local COMMITS=$(command git log @{upstream}..HEAD | grep '^commit' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
added_lines: +    COMMITS=$(command git log @{upstream}..HEAD | grep '^commit' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')

added_lines: +    compls=$(grep -Eo "gulp.task\((['\"](([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)['\"],)" gulpfile.js 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo "['\"](([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)['\"]" | sed s/"['\"]"//g | sort)"

Allow completion for 'composer.phar', not only 'composer'

Use $_comp_command1 for command list generation instead of 'composer':

  This allows us to have a different composer name

`\d` in sed regular expressions doesn't work (see http://stackoverflow.com/questions/14671293/why-does-d-doesnt-work-in-regular-expression-in-sed)

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: Added further cleanup and svn status information
Better filename matching
Fix bad ps syntax in ssh-agent plugin

Fix work_in_progress in empty git repos

I noticed that the function ``work_in_progress``, which is used in the "gallois"-theme, would print ``fatal: bad default revision 'HEAD'`` in a new folder after ``git init``.

This is the fix.

randquote plugin
correcting an issue about encoding some people was having
added a message when the user doesn't have curl
added varibles for colors->easier to customize

Only trim remote names; be git-flow friendly

 - don't remove everything before the last /
 - do remove <remote_name>/
 - allows flow style feature/foobar branch names
removed_lines: -      compadd `git branch -r | grep -v HEAD | sed "s/.*\///" | sed "s/ //g"`
added_lines: +      compadd `git branch -r | grep -v HEAD | sed "s/$remotes\///" | sed "s/ //g"`

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
gitfast: synchronize with upstream

Up to version 1.8.2.1.

Signed-off-by: Felipe Contreras <felipe.contreras@gmail.com>

This fixes checking for git untracked items

The grep expression was incorrect for normal grep. Adding
the `-E` option fixed the escaping of the question marks.

Fix rbenv gems helper

Fixes gem versions with words (beta, pre) in the version or with
multiple installed versions not being highlighted, and compatibility
with GNU sed (related to robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh#1579).

Fix Symfony2 command completion 'permission denied'

app/console by default (if you create a new Symfony project via composer create-project or by downloading it from symfony.com) is not executable. Therefore I get the following error:

sf2 _symfony2_get_command_list:1: permission denied: app/console 
_symfony2_get_command_list:1: permission denied: app/console
_symfony2_get_command_list:1: permission denied: app/console

To make command completion work without changing app/console you just have to let php run it.
added_lines: +	php app/console --no-ansi | sed "1,/Available commands/d" | awk '/^  [a-z]+/ { print $1 }'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: OpenBSD doesn't have -ef flags for ps. Both linux and OpenBSD have -x flags which works just as greate here
Fixed the rfind command in the  ruby plugin

Fix issues with special characters when running mvn

Setting the locale to C will stabilize sed, so it won't stop processing
the mvn output when it encounters invalid characters (like binary data)

This makes it also more viable to add the `alias mvn='mvn-color`, as
the coloring is less obtrusive, and there won't be any issues with sed
breaking because of an invalid character inside the stream

Don't drop everything after a trailing slash, as this breaks standard svn branches:
^/branches/featurename
^/releases/Release-vx.y
^/trunk
fix: rbenv: I need to find a proper way to test changes...

added_lines: +      echo "$(rbenv gemset active 2&>/dev/null | sed -e ":a" -e '$ s/\n/+/gp;N;b a' | head -n1)"
